title: Allow to see multi-arch manifest when using compat listImages API
body:
### Feature request description

If I create a manifest, I'm able to see it with `podman images` and with the libpod API

but not with the compat API

```
$ podman manifest create my-multi-arch-image                                                                                                                                                                         bbbbb22e0d115353760b07d41652235d69c319785baa3aad8d4ce39e9bf25de4
```

```
$ podman image ls
REPOSITORY                                TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE
localhost/my-multi-arch-image             latest      bbbbb22e0d11  4 seconds ago  112 B
```


```
docker images
REPOSITORY   TAG       IMAGE ID       CREATED         SIZE
```


and after building the image using Containerfile being


```Dockerfile
FROM httpd
RUN echo "hello from $(arch)"
```

```
podman build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64  --manifest my-multi-arch-image .
[linux/amd64] STEP 1/2: FROM httpd
Resolving "httpd" using unqualified-search registries (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/999-podman-machine.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/httpd:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:c78cdbf9617d1f5d6de94d728691c4d9d87efd7e0d1ae651034d76879e53e862
Copying blob sha256:8b0a7c8478f88543c0f8c785342abb736016bc8fc281049eaac1de7f897fc854
Copying blob sha256:8a1e25ce7c4f75e372e9884f8f7b1bedcfe4a7a7d452eb4b0a1c7477c9a90345
Copying blob sha256:7f8fb0a042e02e8065725f2e4aedfc168e8c5e37d5e070488daca73ed0499878
Copying blob sha256:91e4b2f2b52acaf9f63647ae64b86e999a528cbcfec377d6377e2f96bbdf5a26
Copying blob sha256:4f4fb700ef54461cfa02571ae0db9a0dc1e0cdb5577484a6d75e68dc38e8acc1
Copying config sha256:ac45b24b92cc0527c6af660679d0701f680a6d4214cf5cf9a147f20127d9685e
Writing manifest to image destination
[linux/amd64] STEP 2/2: RUN echo "hello from $(arch)"
hello from x86_64
[linux/amd64] COMMIT
--> f8130d708703
[linux/arm64] STEP 1/2: FROM httpd
f8130d7087033b6410e0705e4279b0375b976579c1d7dd595ca06cc4892726d2
Resolving "httpd" using unqualified-search registries (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/999-podman-machine.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/httpd:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:6a05de95e3c939da8faf371ceb417adace37559a79280d2673c0f7f50dd1be9e
Copying blob sha256:a76fae73ed380d6d81daf4420d8dbfdba9ea83115d34dac4b4527bf9d83cc690
Copying blob sha256:f6f2bb641d9f3f1745742c54d0e488695c2c63768cfaaa0b77b9fe80abd6d5d5
Copying blob sha256:59f5764b1f6d170ea07ca424965974024a14970ff826e9ffa3489c90dc040c24
Copying blob sha256:4f4fb700ef54461cfa02571ae0db9a0dc1e0cdb5577484a6d75e68dc38e8acc1
Copying blob sha256:d924f51924419b60b659342152d4d3084f71af676faf979bd2195405673ab160
Copying config sha256:daa4e17525d4b582223d096940ce53a59bbd5afe5d77d0f7b34a83c74ba666eb
Writing manifest to image destination
[linux/arm64] STEP 2/2: RUN echo "hello from $(arch)"
hello from aarch64
[linux/arm64] COMMIT
--> 149304272199
14930427219905b3b76f5da13461a7f32d8fcb8fb0d00fd0be5c41faa536bb31
```

manifest is now bigger (as it includes link to my two specific arch images)

```
podman  images                                                                                                                                                                                                         REPOSITORY                     TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED         SIZE
<none>                         <none>      149304272199  12 seconds ago  199 MB
<none>                         <none>      f8130d708703  16 seconds ago  173 MB
localhost/my-multi-arch-image  latest      bbbbb22e0d11  55 seconds ago  1.05 kB
docker.io/library/httpd        latest      daa4e17525d4  2 months ago    199 MB
```

but I still don't see it using the compat API

```
docker images                                                                                                                                                                                                    
REPOSITORY   TAG       IMAGE ID       CREATED         SIZE
<none>       <none>    f8130d708703   6 seconds ago   173MB
httpd        latest    daa4e17525d4   2 months ago    199MB
<none>       <none>    149304272199   2 seconds ago   199MB
```



### Suggest potential solution

A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
That is expected behavior IMO.  The images that are part of the manifest list are displayed on the compat layer but the manifest list itself is not (because it's not an image). I see it like the compat layer not showing pods.

Is this behavior causing issues or is it surprising?

on Podman Desktop we're using compat API to list all images

if you build multi-arch images, then the multi-arch image doesn't show up. (so you don't see it and can't push it etc)

also a lot of tools are using compat API so it won't be displayed in those tools as well.


(for Pods there is a dedicated endpoint you don't see pods using listContainers or listImages, it's a specific REST endpoint)
Are the multi-arch images built via the compat or the libpod API?

I guess it's done using libpod API.

but for example we display all images, whatever if they're built using compat or libpod API
If this is what the Docker Daemon does, we can make this change, but we cannot break Docker compat on the compatibility API.
There is no manifest list in the Podman API as well.

> There is no manifest list in the Podman API as well.

so we are all on the same page, could you show the result of a curl with the podman api and tell us what you'd expect?

so it looks like podman images API return some manifests through image list endpoint
but then how to know that you need to call `image inspect` vs `manifest inspect` on the id being returned

https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#tag/manifests/operation/ManifestCreateLibpod
here you can create, delete, inspect but not list all manifests created ?

so from a client POV, it's really hard to know what is listed, what can we do with the resources, etc

>> There is no manifest list in the Podman API as well.
> so we are all on the same page, could you show the result of a curl with the podman api and tell us what you'd expect?

- having multi-arch images being part of the compat API so a call to `podman image ls` or `docker image ls` return the same list
- having a way to list only the manifests (for now how to know that an id in image list is a manifest and not an image)
- inspect a multi arch image inspect the image of the current arch
closing as compat API won't be changed, will open a feature enhancement to include manifests or tell if image is a manifest in libpod API
